channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Falcons, MOUZ & Spirit Eliminated at ESL One Birmingham 2026
item.content:encoded:
Falcons, Mouz, and Spirit were eliminated on the first day of the Arena at ESL One’s Dota 2 event in Birmingham.

Falcons and Mouz lost their Lower Bracket Round 1 matches, before a barnburning contest between Xtreme Gaming and Spirit resulted in Xtreme claiming a startling 2-1 victory.

MOUZ and Falcons lose in Lower Bracket Round 1

MOUZ didn’t have a single drawn Bo2 in the Group Stage; it was either 2-0 or 0-2 for an overall 4-0-3 series record.

Therefore, things were looking very good for MOUZ when they took Game 1 extremely comfortably against an Xtreme that only won one series Bo2 series in Group B.

However, Xtreme were not to be beaten easily with Wang ‘Ame’ Chunyu’s Weaver obliterating MOUZ in Game 2 behind a 15/2/15 statline.

MOUZ had no answer in Game 3, unable to even kill Ame (or Lin ‘Xxs’ Jing) as XG converted the series with Cheng ‘NothingToSay’ Jin Xiang shining on Puch.

MOUZ continued their disappointing run after PGL Wallachia Season 6 victory. Falcons were much the same in a despondent 0-2 loss to Parivision.

Parivision continued their pattern of no pattern. That is to say that Clement ‘Puppey’ Ivanov’s drafts were unreadable to Falcons who had no answers once in the server.

While Falcons fail to find answers in the new patch, Parivision seems revived by it as they continue on to their Lower Bracket Round 2 match against Aurora.

Xtreme outlasts Spirit in Birmingham

Xtreme Gaming’s day was not over, with their Spirit in their way in Lower Bracket Round 2. Spirit was sent there after a 2-1 series loss to Yandex owing to a slow start.

That threatened to happen again as Xtreme started better, with Puck and Gyro starting strong. But it wasn’t enough to overcome Spirit’s classic late-game fightback as they slowly picked Xtreme off before they scaled enough to run them over.

Game 2 saw NothingToSay on Leshrac deny the threat of Spirit’s late-game surge, with a huge fight at 25-minutes setting up the Roshan and springboarding them to a 42-minute victory.

The deciding game was nothing like the previous two. In an hour-long contest, both teams fought tooth and nail to stay in the competition

Ame, Xxs, and NothingToSay above all had significant advantages early in the game, leading by 8k at 15 minutes, but the Chinese roster made poor mistakes to give away a kill each, letting Spirit back in.

A huge Aegis power play restored the lead, with Ame decimating Spirit for a 3-0 teamfight. Still, Xtreme couldn’t shake Spirit, claiming big money kills against Ame to keep it competitive.

Illya ‘Yatoro’ Mulyarchuk’s Morphling thought that it was time to activate, but a series of fights left Spirit baited in to trying to steal the Roshan, allowing Xtreme to claim a decisive teamfight win even with missing out on the Aegis.

Xtreme removed the top and mid defences, but an Ame overstep soon reversed their fortunes, and suddenly it was Spirit putting the pressure on.

Spirit then forced the fight onto Ame, they got the kill, but the buyback came through, resulting in four Xtreme members living in the resultant chaos, and only Denis ‘Larl’ Sigitov living for Spirit.

Despite Xtreme’s lead, the game devolved into a buyback war. Waiting for buyback, one teamfight exhausts them, wait again.

Finally, poor positioning from Larl led to the decisive moment, with Xtreme finally exhausting the buybacks and dealing true deaths to the Spirit roster, eliminating the clear favourites for the series.

Ultimately, after losing the early game, Spirit were waiting for the mistake from Xtreme, but it came from their side of the map.

Can Falcons recover without a roster change? Let us know on our Discord server!

For more Dota 2 action, see our interview with Yandex’s watson.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

https://insider-gaming.com/esl-one-birmingham-arena-day-1-catchup/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Strands #754 Hints and Answers for Today, March 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #752 for Friday, March 27, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 27, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #754 are all terms to describe ideal situations. Think of your favorite descriptors for the best outcome, and you’ll be good to go.

General Strands Hints For March 27

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Curt Hennig’s WWF moniker.
  • Hint 2: _____-o-mundo
  • Hint 3: When you go to someone to make your clothes fit exactly right, they are this.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #754 for March 27, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 27, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • RAGE
  • SORT
  • EASE
  • PEAT
  • DEAL

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #754 On March 27, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • PERFECT
  • EXACT
  • IDEAL
  • SEEMLY
  • FITTING
  • SUITABLE
  • SPANAGRAM: TAILORMADE

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #754?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 28 will be Strands Game #755.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-754-hints-and-answers-for-today-march-27-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Slay the Spire 2 Beta Patch Reverts Major Changes
item.content:encoded:
Slay the Spire 2 recently released patch notes for its upcoming Beta Patch. The previous one, although optional, received thousands of negative reviews from players. This new patch reverses some of the changes from the previous update.

Along with general bug fixes and balance adjustments, Slay the Spire 2 v0.101.0 introduces numerous changes. With these updates, a message from one of the developers reiterates that nothing is final at this stage and that feedback is still necessary.

In the Steam announcement, several insights were provided to explain the changes. For example, the changes made to Prepared, Borrowed Time, and Capture Spirit have all been reverted from last week’s update.

“For Prepared, while I still think that the Sly synergies are overall too dominant a strategy, Prepared is so integral to the Silent’s core identity that I will be looking for a different approach to bring Sly in line in the future. Borrowed Time is likely to receive a complete rework in the future, but for now, I prefer that the card is fun to play with in all decks,” the update notes.

User feedback was also emphasized later in the patch notes, as one of the major updates is an increased feedback form character limit from 500 to 8,000. That’s more than a tenfold increase.

“Receiving in-game feedback from YOU, the player, is the best way we have of improving the game. Many of you have requested the ability to write more without hitting the character cap too early. We’ve increased the character count 16-fold, so I am eagerly looking forward to reading your effort posts,” it’s written.

Check out the full patch notes at this link. Insider Gaming will continue to bring the latest news regarding the development of Slay the Spire 2.

Are you currently playing the deckbuilder? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
